annotation.text:
This example stream demonstrates how the fictional company Botbot meters AI chatbot usage to bill customers per API call.

Botbot's AI Assist product has two chatbot tiers: a cheaper L1 for basic questions and a more expensive L2 for business-specific support, and customers are charged for the API calls made to each tier per session.

The stream shows how to configure multiple meter types under shared categories, use the Meter processor with multiple guiding rules, and create meters when a customer activates a chatbot service.

In production, the meter types and meters would be set up via API; in this example, they are configured by helper streams you run alongside this stream.
Send the metered L1 and L2 API call summaries to a downstream billing system.
The Meter's threshold output is not used in this example.
Generate sample data representing customer service bot interactions, indicating the number of API calls made to each bot type.
Ensure that no duplicate sessions are processed and that all regions are valid.
Formats data for metering per user and month, with L1 and L2 API calls metered separately. These monthly summaries are ideal for invoice line items.
